journey_statuses.proven: A pinned checkpoint has a checked-in, reproducible MLX proof packet in this repository.
journey_statuses.simulation: A teaching route assembled from canonical runbooks and evidence; it is not a completed checkpoint port.

foundations.title: What MLX is
foundations.plain_language: MLX is an array framework for machine learning on Apple silicon with a NumPy-like API.
foundations.why_it_matters: Its lazy execution and unified-memory model change where porters place evaluation, state, and measurement boundaries.
foundations.pytorch_cuda: PyTorch projects commonly think in eager tensors plus explicit CPU-to-CUDA movement.
foundations.mlx_translation: Build with mx.array and mlx.nn, let operations select device and stream, and evaluate only at deliberate boundaries.
foundations.example: Create arrays and modules first; call mx.eval only when a dependency, comparison, or measurement needs realized results.
foundations.common_trap: Treating unified memory as a promise that every framework bridge is zero-copy.
foundations.proof_check: You can explain when an MLX expression is built and when its result is actually needed.
foundations.next_step: Learn what a port must preserve beyond weights.

foundations.title: What porting means
foundations.plain_language: A port reproduces a source model's behavior in a new runtime.
foundations.why_it_matters: Checkpoint conversion alone cannot preserve control flow, layouts, masks, cache semantics, preprocessing, or generation behavior.
foundations.pytorch_cuda: The source implementation and its outputs are the behavioral contract, not only state_dict keys.
foundations.mlx_translation: Rebuild the computation in MLX, transform every weight explicitly, and compare staged outputs against a source oracle.
foundations.example: Match tokenizer inputs, one primitive, one block, full logits, stateful decode, and the task result in order.
foundations.common_trap: Loading tensors successfully and calling the model ported before output parity exists.
foundations.proof_check: You can name source semantics, weight transforms, state, and outputs that need evidence.
foundations.next_step: Read the model before choosing an implementation route.

foundations.title: Read the model before code
foundations.plain_language: Inventory the model's artifacts, inputs, outputs, repeated blocks, state, and surrounding pipeline.
foundations.why_it_matters: Modality, architecture, and workload are separate decisions that select different runbooks and proof gates.
foundations.pytorch_cuda: A Transformers class name may hide a vision tower, audio frontend, decoder, scheduler, or custom cache.
foundations.mlx_translation: Route every component through its native MLX family and record uncertainty instead of forcing one label.
foundations.example: Whisper is speech input and an encoder-decoder architecture; LLaVA composes vision, projection, and language components.
foundations.common_trap: Using modality as the architecture family or treating a composed model as one homogeneous block.
foundations.proof_check: The port plan lists source format, modality, architecture components, state, preprocessing, and output contract.
foundations.next_step: Capture a source oracle before rewriting anything.

foundations.title: The correctness rail
foundations.plain_language: Move from the smallest comparable value to the complete task, stopping at the first divergence.
foundations.why_it_matters: A late end-to-end failure gives too little information to locate a semantic mismatch.
foundations.pytorch_cuda: Capture deterministic source tensors and state at named checkpoints.
foundations.mlx_translation: Implement an eager readable MLX path, map weights, then compare primitive, block, output, state, and task rungs.
foundations.example: A wrong attention mask should fail at attention output before it is hidden by later residual blocks.
foundations.common_trap: Optimizing or compiling before an eager path has parity.
foundations.proof_check: Every passed rung names inputs, shapes, dtype policy, tolerance, and source artifact.
foundations.next_step: Learn how to debug the first failed rung.

foundations.title: Debug the first divergence
foundations.plain_language: The earliest failing comparison is the highest-signal place to investigate.
foundations.why_it_matters: Later errors compound and can make a local layout or state bug look like a global model failure.
foundations.pytorch_cuda: Inspect source shapes, layouts, dtypes, masks, position rules, and state mutation at the failing boundary.
foundations.mlx_translation: Compare the matching MLX primitive, then the enclosing block, output head, recurrent/cache state, and task result.
foundations.example: If projected vision embeddings match but logits do not, inspect token placement and decoder inputs next.
foundations.common_trap: Loosening tolerances until the failure disappears without explaining the numerical policy.
foundations.proof_check: A fix makes the intended rung pass while all earlier rungs remain green.
foundations.next_step: Profile only after the correctness rail is stable.

foundations.title: Profile the real bottleneck
foundations.plain_language: Measure the phase that limits the target workload before choosing an optimization.
foundations.why_it_matters: Prefill, decode, preprocessing, denoising, streaming, memory, and serving can demand different techniques.
foundations.pytorch_cuda: CUDA intuition about a hot kernel does not prove the same bottleneck exists on MLX and Apple silicon.
foundations.mlx_translation: Place evaluation around measured regions so lazy work is included exactly once and synchronization is intentional.
foundations.example: Separate prompt prefill from token decode instead of reporting one blended generation time.
foundations.common_trap: Timing graph construction while the computation is realized outside the measured region.
foundations.proof_check: The receipt names workload, cold or warm state, evaluation boundaries, hardware, software, and quality gate.
foundations.next_step: Select a technique from the measured bottleneck branch.

foundations.title: Benchmark without fooling yourself
foundations.plain_language: A benchmark is a scoped observation, not a portable promise.
foundations.why_it_matters: Shape, cache state, dtype, model revision, quality, and measurement boundaries can change the result.
foundations.pytorch_cuda: Framework benchmark habits still need explicit warmup, synchronization, inputs, and quality checks.
foundations.mlx_translation: Force the intended lazy work inside the timed region and preserve raw evidence plus rollback conditions.
foundations.example: Compare the same prompt, decode length, cache state, dtype policy, and output-quality gate.
foundations.common_trap: Publishing a source-reported or local speed range as an effective recommendation.
foundations.proof_check: Another engineer can reproduce the exact workload and see why the number is or is not promotion-ready.
foundations.next_step: Package the port and its proof boundary together.

foundations.title: Publish the proof boundary
foundations.plain_language: Ship the implementation together with provenance, validation, limitations, and a rollback story.
foundations.why_it_matters: Users need to know exactly which checkpoint, workload, and behavior were reproduced.
foundations.pytorch_cuda: A converted artifact without its source revision, transforms, and validation cannot be audited.
foundations.mlx_translation: Record source pin, weight map, parity report, benchmark metadata, supported route, and known gaps.
foundations.example: Label one pinned Qwen checkpoint proven while keeping neighboring Qwen architectures unclaimed.
foundations.common_trap: Promoting indexed research or a passing smoke test into supported guidance.
foundations.proof_check: Every claim points to evidence and every risky change names a rollback condition.
foundations.next_step: Use the field manual to execute the same rail on a real model.

translation_lens.title: Arrays and device placement
translation_lens.plain_language: MLX arrays live in unified memory while operations select a device and stream.
translation_lens.why_it_matters: Ported code should not copy PyTorch device-transfer choreography blindly.
translation_lens.pytorch_cuda: torch.Tensor plus .to('cuda') or explicit host/device copies.
translation_lens.mlx_translation: Use mx.array and operation or stream placement; treat framework interop as a separate boundary.
translation_lens.example: Keep the array identity stable and choose the GPU stream for the operation that consumes it.
translation_lens.common_trap: Assuming every external buffer shares storage without a copy.
translation_lens.proof_check: The same input produces the expected shape, dtype, and values on the selected MLX device.
translation_lens.next_step: Account for lazy evaluation before comparing timing.

translation_lens.title: Eager assumptions to lazy evaluation
translation_lens.plain_language: MLX builds computation graphs lazily and realizes them when results are needed.
translation_lens.why_it_matters: Evaluation placement affects memory, timing, and when state becomes observable.
translation_lens.pytorch_cuda: Most eager PyTorch operations execute as they are issued.
translation_lens.mlx_translation: Use deliberate mx.eval boundaries at comparisons, dependencies, and measurements.
translation_lens.example: Evaluate model outputs inside a timed region before stopping the timer.
translation_lens.common_trap: Printing, scalar extraction, or NumPy conversion creates an accidental synchronization point.
translation_lens.proof_check: Timing includes the intended work once and parity reads realized outputs.

translation_lens.title: state_dict to weight map
translation_lens.plain_language: Weights need a deterministic name, shape, layout, and dtype transformation contract.
translation_lens.why_it_matters: Matching parameter counts does not prove tensors land in semantically equivalent operations.
translation_lens.pytorch_cuda: Load a state_dict into a source class with framework-native naming and layouts.
translation_lens.mlx_translation: Declare rename, transpose, reshape, split, merge, and dtype transforms before assignment.
translation_lens.example: Split a fused QKV tensor only when the source layout and target attention module require it.
translation_lens.common_trap: Guessing a transpose from a coincidentally compatible shape.
translation_lens.proof_check: Source-key coverage is complete and every transformed tensor has a shape and checksum record.

translation_lens.title: Framework layout to operation layout
translation_lens.plain_language: Layout is an operation contract, not a global framework preference.
translation_lens.why_it_matters: Image, attention, convolution, and linear operations may expect different axis orders.
translation_lens.pytorch_cuda: Source code may rely on NCHW conventions or implicit contiguous tensors.
translation_lens.mlx_translation: Make every required transpose and reshape visible at the boundary that needs it.
translation_lens.example: Record whether attention uses batch, heads, query, and key dimensions before calling fast SDPA.
translation_lens.common_trap: Applying one repository-wide layout rule to every modality.
translation_lens.proof_check: The primitive comparison passes with named axes and no unexplained reshape.

translation_lens.title: Autocast habits to numerical roles
translation_lens.plain_language: Choose dtype and accumulation by the numerical role of each operation.
translation_lens.why_it_matters: Norms, softmax, recurrence, FFTs, and quantizer distances can be more sensitive than linear layers.
translation_lens.pytorch_cuda: Global AMP or autocast may select mixed precision implicitly.
translation_lens.mlx_translation: Set weight, activation, accumulation, and sensitive-operation dtypes deliberately.
translation_lens.example: Keep a sensitive reduction in higher precision while storing linear weights more compactly.
translation_lens.common_trap: Calling a full-model dtype cast a lossless optimization.
translation_lens.proof_check: Parity tolerances and task quality remain within the declared numerical policy.

translation_lens.title: Hidden mutation to explicit state
translation_lens.plain_language: Caches and recurrent values should be visible inputs and outputs of the ported computation.
translation_lens.why_it_matters: Hidden mutation can make parity, compilation, and reuse incorrect or stale.
translation_lens.pytorch_cuda: Modules may mutate buffers, KV caches, or recurrent fields internally.
translation_lens.mlx_translation: Thread cache, recurrent, and compiled state explicitly and validate update order.
translation_lens.example: Compare one-token KV growth and the next-token logits from the same prior state.
translation_lens.common_trap: Capturing mutable state inside a compiled function and reusing stale values.
translation_lens.proof_check: State shapes, positions, contents, and reset semantics pass staged comparisons.

translation_lens.title: torch.compile and CUDA graphs to mx.compile
translation_lens.plain_language: Compile pure, stable, reused MLX regions after eager parity.
translation_lens.why_it_matters: Changing shapes, dtypes, Python structure, or captured state can retrace or invalidate assumptions.
translation_lens.pytorch_cuda: torch.compile and CUDA graphs optimize or capture framework execution under their own contracts.
translation_lens.mlx_translation: Use mx.compile for a function with explicit inputs, outputs, and stable structure.
translation_lens.example: Compile a repeated denoiser step while leaving scheduler bookkeeping explicit.
translation_lens.common_trap: Compiling an entire dynamic pipeline before locating the measured bottleneck.
translation_lens.proof_check: Compiled and eager outputs match and the target workload shows an end-to-end benefit.
translation_lens.next_step: Escalate to custom code only when profiling justifies it.

translation_lens.title: CUDA extensions to native MLX or Metal
translation_lens.plain_language: Prefer readable MLX operations before custom backend work.
translation_lens.why_it_matters: A custom kernel adds correctness, portability, maintenance, and fallback obligations.
translation_lens.pytorch_cuda: A source project may depend on a CUDA extension or fused kernel.
translation_lens.mlx_translation: Try native MLX operations, mx.fast, and compilation first; use custom Metal only after profiling.
translation_lens.example: Replace compatible attention with MLX fast SDPA before designing an attention kernel.
translation_lens.common_trap: Porting a CUDA optimization before reproducing the source semantics it accelerates.
translation_lens.proof_check: The custom path matches the readable fallback and names a no-benefit rollback condition.

translation_lens.title: CUDA timing to MLX evaluation boundaries
translation_lens.plain_language: Lazy scheduling means timing must force the work being measured.
translation_lens.why_it_matters: A timer around graph construction can omit execution or charge it to the next phase.
translation_lens.pytorch_cuda: CUDA measurements commonly use explicit synchronization or framework events.
translation_lens.mlx_translation: Evaluate outputs at the intended dependency or measurement boundary and avoid unrelated sync points.
translation_lens.example: Realize prefill logits before ending the prefill timer, then time cached decode separately.
translation_lens.common_trap: Comparing an eager source measurement with an unevaluated MLX expression.
translation_lens.proof_check: Repeated cold and warm runs account for the same work and state.
translation_lens.next_step: Use framework bridges only as controlled oracle tools.

translation_lens.title: Framework bridges for oracle comparison
translation_lens.plain_language: NumPy and DLPack can move comparable values across framework boundaries.
translation_lens.why_it_matters: They are useful for proof but can force evaluation, copy memory, or change ownership semantics.
translation_lens.pytorch_cuda: Convert source tensors to a neutral or exchange representation for comparison.
translation_lens.mlx_translation: Use documented conversion paths and keep the bridge outside measured model regions.
translation_lens.example: Export one source checkpoint tensor for a deterministic primitive comparison.
translation_lens.common_trap: Including conversion time in one runtime but not the other.
translation_lens.proof_check: The receipt records copy, dtype, ownership, and synchronization behavior at the boundary.
translation_lens.next_step: Return to native MLX arrays for the actual port.

checkpoint_nodes.title: Inspect
checkpoint_nodes.concept: Identify artifacts, inputs, outputs, modality, architecture, state, and source format.
checkpoint_nodes.why_mlx_differs: MLX route selection depends on the actual computation, not a filename or task label.
checkpoint_nodes.inspect: Config, class graph, tensor inventory, preprocessing, generation or scheduler code, and custom operations.
checkpoint_nodes.prerequisite: A pinned local source revision with remote model code disabled.
checkpoint_nodes.proof: The port plan records evidence and uncertainty for every selected family.

checkpoint_nodes.title: Capture oracle
checkpoint_nodes.concept: Record source tensors, state, and outputs before translating implementation details.
checkpoint_nodes.why_mlx_differs: The source oracle is the behavioral contract used to judge the new runtime.
checkpoint_nodes.inspect: Inputs, RNG, preprocessing, primitive boundaries, repeated blocks, state, logits, and task output.
checkpoint_nodes.prerequisite: A safe source runtime and a small representative input.
checkpoint_nodes.proof: Captured artifacts are bounded, named, reproducible, and tied to the source revision.

checkpoint_nodes.title: Rebuild
checkpoint_nodes.concept: Reproduce source semantics with native MLX modules and operations.
checkpoint_nodes.why_mlx_differs: Lazy execution, layout, state, and numerical roles need explicit MLX choices.
checkpoint_nodes.inspect: Operator semantics, masks, positions, residual order, activation functions, state, and output contracts.
checkpoint_nodes.proof: Small synthetic inputs exercise every critical branch before real weights are loaded.

checkpoint_nodes.title: Map weights
checkpoint_nodes.concept: Declare how every source tensor becomes a target parameter.
checkpoint_nodes.why_mlx_differs: Names, layouts, fused projections, and dtype policies can differ from the source framework.
checkpoint_nodes.inspect: Rename, transpose, reshape, split, merge, dtype, destination shape, and source-key coverage.
checkpoint_nodes.prerequisite: Target module tree and a complete source tensor inventory.
checkpoint_nodes.proof: The converter reports complete coverage, expected shapes, and no unexplained tensor drop.

checkpoint_nodes.title: Prove parity
checkpoint_nodes.concept: Compare staged values from primitives through the final task.
checkpoint_nodes.why_mlx_differs: Only parity distinguishes a semantic port from a checkpoint that merely loads.
checkpoint_nodes.inspect: Shapes, primitive outputs, blocks, final outputs, cache or recurrent state, and task behavior.
checkpoint_nodes.prerequisite: Source oracle, eager implementation, and complete weight map.
checkpoint_nodes.proof: Every rung passes a declared tolerance or exact-match rule; the first divergence is explained.

checkpoint_nodes.title: Profile
checkpoint_nodes.concept: Separate the workload into phases and find the limiting one.
checkpoint_nodes.why_mlx_differs: Lazy scheduling and unified memory change where work and synchronization are observed.
checkpoint_nodes.inspect: Prefill, decode, preprocessing, repeated steps, memory, streaming, synchronization, and serving behavior.
checkpoint_nodes.prerequisite: A parity-passing eager path and representative workload.
checkpoint_nodes.proof: The receipt includes evaluation boundaries, hardware, software, shapes, warm state, and quality.

checkpoint_nodes.title: Optimize
checkpoint_nodes.outcome: A faster or smaller path with proof
checkpoint_nodes.concept: Choose a technique that matches the measured bottleneck and architecture.
checkpoint_nodes.why_mlx_differs: Native operators, compilation, caches, compression, algorithms, and serving each have different contracts.
checkpoint_nodes.inspect: Applicability, prerequisite, evidence status, quality gate, benchmark, and rollback.
checkpoint_nodes.prerequisite: Parity and a measured bottleneck; this node is gated until both exist.
checkpoint_nodes.proof: Parity and quality remain valid and the representative end-to-end workload improves.

checkpoint_nodes.title: Publish
checkpoint_nodes.concept: Package code, provenance, transforms, validation, measurements, limitations, and rollback.
checkpoint_nodes.why_mlx_differs: A reusable MLX route needs an honest boundary around model, target, and evidence.
checkpoint_nodes.inspect: Source pin, license, plan, weight map, parity report, benchmark receipts, runbook, and known gaps.
checkpoint_nodes.prerequisite: All claims have canonical evidence and unsupported paths remain labeled.
checkpoint_nodes.proof: A clean environment can reproduce the documented route without remote model code.

journeys.title: Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct
journeys.summary: The only guided lab backed by a checked-in checkpoint port and ordered parity packet.
journeys.proof_boundary: Claim one pinned local real-model port, exact eight-token greedy agreement for the F32 artifact, and one held F32-versus-BF16 wall-time observation. Do not generalize the tolerance, BF16 quality window, performance ratio, longer-context behavior, batching, or publication readiness.

component_path.title: Tokenizer contract
component_path.concept: Prompt formatting and tokenization define the first model input.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: The MLX graph can be correct while a different chat template changes every later value.
component_path.inspect: Tokenizer revision, special tokens, template, padding, and input IDs.
component_path.prerequisite: Pinned model and tokenizer artifacts.
component_path.proof: The source and target consume the same token IDs.

component_path.title: Embedding
component_path.concept: The embedding table maps token IDs into the model width.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: Weight naming and gather semantics must match before repeated blocks are meaningful.
component_path.inspect: Vocabulary size, hidden size, tied weights, dtype, and gather output.
component_path.prerequisite: Exact token IDs and mapped embedding weight.
component_path.proof: Embedding outputs pass the declared tensor comparison.

component_path.title: Decoder block loop
component_path.outcome: Attention and MLP preserve source order
component_path.concept: Repeated RMSNorm, RoPE, grouped-query attention, residual, and MLP blocks transform the sequence.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: Mask shape, RoPE convention, GQA grouping, residual order, and lazy state must stay explicit.
component_path.inspect: Norm epsilon, projection layout, head counts, RoPE base, mask, activation, and residual order.
component_path.prerequisite: Primitive-level parity for norm, RoPE, attention, and MLP.
component_path.proof: Every ordered block checkpoint passes before the final head is compared.

component_path.title: KV cache state
component_path.concept: Key and value state grows across autoregressive generation.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: Cache layout, position offsets, update order, and evaluation boundaries are target-runtime choices.
component_path.inspect: Cache axes, length, grouped heads, positions, reset, and next-token logits.
component_path.prerequisite: Full prefill parity and explicit cache state.
component_path.proof: Single-token cached decode matches the source at each checked step.

component_path.title: Norm, head, and generation
component_path.concept: Final normalization and the language head produce logits used by the generation loop.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: Tied weights, sampling order, cache reuse, and stop rules can diverge after tensor parity.
component_path.inspect: Final norm, head weight, logits, greedy selection, stop tokens, and emitted IDs.
component_path.prerequisite: All earlier parity rungs and cache checks pass.
component_path.proof: The checked-in packet records matching inputs and the exact greedy output sequence.

checkpoint_notes.inspect: Pin the exact Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct revision and identify the dense decoder route.
checkpoint_notes.oracle: Capture tokenizer IDs, primitive tensors, repeated blocks, logits, KV state, and greedy outputs.
checkpoint_notes.implement: Build a readable standalone MLX decoder with explicit RoPE, GQA, RMSNorm, masks, and cache.
checkpoint_notes.map: Apply the checked-in schema-2 rename and transform contract with full source-key coverage.
checkpoint_notes.parity: Run the ordered 29-rung ladder under its declared floating-point policy.
checkpoint_notes.profile: Measure load, prefill, and cached decode separately; keep observations tied to the local receipt.
checkpoint_notes.optimize: Only after parity, test attention, compile, dtype, quantization, cache, or decode-algorithm branches independently.
checkpoint_notes.publish: Publish the exact checkpoint boundary, parity packet, receipts, and limitations without generalizing to all Qwen models.

journeys.title: Whisper-style ASR
journeys.summary: A runbook simulation for audio preprocessing, encoder-decoder structure, cross-attention, cache, timestamps, and task quality.
journeys.proof_boundary: This is a runbook simulation, not a completed Whisper checkpoint port. The official MLX example proves an implementation path exists; it does not provide this repository's pinned parity and task-quality packet.

component_path.title: Waveform contract
component_path.concept: Sample rate, channel policy, normalization, clipping, and duration define the raw input.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: A model graph cannot correct a frontend mismatch introduced before MLX sees the samples.
component_path.inspect: Sample rate, channel fold, dtype, amplitude, padding, and truncation.
component_path.prerequisite: Pinned audio fixture and preprocessing configuration.
component_path.proof: The same normalized waveform reaches both frontends.

component_path.title: Mel frontend
component_path.concept: Windowing, FFT, mel filters, log scaling, and padding create encoder features.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: FFT and layout details can diverge before any learned layer runs.
component_path.inspect: Window, hop, FFT size, mel basis, log floor, frame count, layout, and dtype.
component_path.prerequisite: Exact waveform contract and source frontend oracle.
component_path.proof: Mel features pass shape and value gates under an explicit tolerance.

component_path.title: Audio encoder
component_path.concept: The encoder turns mel frames into contextual audio representations.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: Convolution layout, positions, attention masks, and normalization need explicit translation.
component_path.inspect: Frontend convolutions, positions, encoder masks, attention, MLP, and norms.
component_path.prerequisite: Feature parity and mapped encoder weights.
component_path.proof: Selected encoder blocks and the final encoder output pass parity.

component_path.title: Cross-attention decoder
component_path.outcome: Text state attends to audio correctly
component_path.concept: The decoder mixes text self-attention with cross-attention over encoder features.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: Self and cross caches have different lifetimes, masks, positions, and update rules.
component_path.inspect: Decoder prompt tokens, self mask, cross mask, cache split, forced tokens, and language/task controls.
component_path.prerequisite: Encoder output parity and explicit cache structures.
component_path.proof: Decoder checkpoints and next-token logits match for the same state.

component_path.title: Transcript and timestamps
component_path.concept: Token decoding, suppression rules, timestamps, and text normalization create the user-visible output.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: Matching logits is necessary but not sufficient for identical transcript and timestamp behavior.
component_path.inspect: Decoding options, suppressed tokens, timestamp rules, normalization, WER or CER, and segment output.
component_path.prerequisite: Encoder-decoder parity on a representative corpus slice.
component_path.proof: Task metrics and timestamp behavior meet a declared quality gate.

checkpoint_notes.inspect: Classify speech modality separately from the encoder-decoder architecture and frontend.
checkpoint_notes.oracle: Capture waveform, mel features, encoder outputs, cross-attention decoder checkpoints, and timestamps.
checkpoint_notes.implement: Rebuild the frontend, audio encoder, text decoder, masks, positions, and distinct cache lifetimes.
checkpoint_notes.map: Map convolution, encoder, decoder, cross-attention, embedding, and head tensors explicitly.
checkpoint_notes.parity: Prove features, encoder, decoder, state, logits, transcript, and timestamp behavior in order.
checkpoint_notes.profile: Separate frontend, encoder, autoregressive decoder, and task post-processing costs.
checkpoint_notes.optimize: For offline Whisper, start with attention, compilation, and evaluation boundaries only when their prerequisites hold.
checkpoint_notes.publish: Keep this route labeled simulation until a pinned checkpoint and task-quality packet are reproduced.

journeys.title: FLUX-style diffusion/flow
journeys.summary: A generic FLUX-style simulation for multi-component pipelines and repeated flow or denoising steps; it is not an exact FLUX port claim.
journeys.proof_boundary: This is a generic FLUX-style teaching route, not an exact FLUX checkpoint claim. Fixed-seed output still requires declared image-quality checks; pixel identity is not promised by default.

component_path.title: Prompt encoder
component_path.concept: Tokenizer and text encoder outputs condition the image-generation process.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: A pipeline may contain separately versioned encoders with their own layouts and dtype policies.
component_path.inspect: Tokenizer, encoder variants, hidden-state selection, pooling, masks, and guidance inputs.
component_path.prerequisite: Pinned pipeline configuration and prompt fixture.
component_path.proof: Conditioning tensors match before entering the denoiser.

component_path.title: Latent and scheduler
component_path.concept: Initial latent, timestep or sigma schedule, guidance, and RNG define the iterative path.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: Different RNG, scheduler math, or timestep dtype changes every later latent.
component_path.inspect: Seed, latent shape, scheduler configuration, timestep sequence, scaling, and guidance.
component_path.prerequisite: Pinned pipeline config and deterministic source execution.
component_path.proof: Initial latent and every scheduled input agree under the declared policy.

component_path.title: Repeated flow or denoiser step
component_path.concept: A large transformer or denoiser predicts the update applied at each scheduled step.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: Layout, multimodal conditioning, timestep embedding, attention, and lazy graph lifetime shape the hot loop.
component_path.inspect: Block order, conditioning joins, attention, timestep embedding, residuals, and output parameterization.
component_path.prerequisite: Matching latent, schedule, and conditioning tensors.
component_path.proof: Selected internal blocks and one full update pass parity before iteration.

component_path.title: Latent trajectory
component_path.concept: The scheduler repeatedly combines predictions and latents.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: Small numerical or scheduler differences accumulate over the entire trajectory.
component_path.inspect: Latent checkpoints at fixed steps, evaluation boundaries, dtype, and scheduler state.
component_path.prerequisite: One-step parity and identical schedule.
component_path.proof: Fixed-step latent checkpoints satisfy the declared comparison policy.

component_path.title: VAE decode and image
component_path.concept: The final latent is decoded and post-processed into an image.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: VAE layouts, scaling, clipping, color conversion, and quality metrics sit outside the denoiser.
component_path.inspect: Latent scaling, decoder, output range, channel order, post-processing, and quality metric.
component_path.prerequisite: Latent trajectory parity and mapped VAE weights.
component_path.proof: Fixed-seed output and declared image-quality checks pass without claiming pixel identity by default.

checkpoint_notes.inspect: Inventory every pipeline component, scheduler, source artifact, and conditioning path.
checkpoint_notes.oracle: Capture conditioning, initial latent, schedule, selected denoiser blocks, latent steps, and final decode.
checkpoint_notes.implement: Rebuild components independently, then compose a readable single-step pipeline before iteration.
checkpoint_notes.map: Keep encoder, denoiser, and VAE weight namespaces and transforms separately auditable.
checkpoint_notes.parity: Compare scheduler identity, one-step updates, fixed latent checkpoints, VAE output, and quality.
checkpoint_notes.profile: Separate prompt encoding, repeated denoiser work, scheduler overhead, transfers, and VAE decode.
checkpoint_notes.optimize: Current registry candidates are stable compilation, evaluation boundaries, block streaming, and the spatial grid-sample kernel.
checkpoint_notes.publish: Label the route FLUX-style simulation until an exact model source and reproducible MLX proof are checked in.

journeys.title: LLaVA-style vision-language model
journeys.summary: A composed-route simulation that keeps image processing, vision encoding, projection, language decoding, and KV state visible.
journeys.proof_boundary: This is a composed-route simulation with no pinned model outcome or local proof ladder. MLX-VLM evidence is third-party and must not be presented as Apple-official or locally reproduced.

component_path.title: Image processor
component_path.concept: Resize, crop, normalize, tile, and patch policies create the vision input.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: A correct vision encoder cannot repair different pixels or patch layout.
component_path.inspect: Image size, aspect policy, interpolation, normalization, tiling, channel order, and batch shape.
component_path.prerequisite: Pinned processor configuration and image fixture.
component_path.proof: Processed pixels and metadata match the source contract.

component_path.title: Vision encoder
component_path.concept: An encoder transformer turns image patches into visual features.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: Patch layout, position encoding, attention, feature selection, and dtype must be routed through the encoder family.
component_path.inspect: Patch embedding, positions, masks, encoder blocks, selected layers, and output layout.
component_path.prerequisite: Pixel parity and mapped vision weights.
component_path.proof: Selected vision blocks and final feature tensors pass parity.

component_path.title: Multimodal projector
component_path.concept: A projector maps vision width and token structure into the language model embedding space.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: Projection layout, activation, feature selection, and image-token count determine language positions.
component_path.inspect: Input layer selection, projector architecture, activation, output width, and token count.
component_path.prerequisite: Vision feature parity and mapped projector weights.
component_path.proof: Projected embeddings match before they are inserted into the text sequence.

component_path.title: Multimodal token assembly
component_path.concept: Projected image tokens replace or join placeholders in the language sequence.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: Template, placeholder count, padding, positions, and masks can shift the entire decoder state.
component_path.inspect: Prompt template, image placeholders, insertion index, attention mask, positions, and labels.
component_path.prerequisite: Projected embedding and tokenizer parity.
component_path.proof: Assembled embeddings, positions, and masks match exactly where required.

component_path.title: Language decoder and output
component_path.concept: A dense decoder with KV state generates text conditioned on visual tokens.
component_path.why_mlx_differs: Decoder attention, cache, image-prefix reuse, sampling, and serving state are separate contracts.
component_path.inspect: Prefill logits, cached decode, positions, stop rules, text output, and image-conditioned task quality.
component_path.prerequisite: All modality-boundary and decoder checkpoints pass.
component_path.proof: Logits, cache behavior, and a declared multimodal task gate pass for the pinned scenario.

checkpoint_notes.inspect: Route image processing, the encoder tower, projector, dense decoder, and KV state as separate components.
checkpoint_notes.oracle: Capture pixels, vision features, projected embeddings, token assembly, logits, cache, and final output.
checkpoint_notes.implement: Rebuild each component through its native runbook before composing the multimodal path.
checkpoint_notes.map: Keep vision, projector, and language tensor transforms distinct with complete source-key coverage.
checkpoint_notes.parity: Prove every modality boundary before evaluating the final image-conditioned task.
checkpoint_notes.profile: Separate image processing, vision encoding, projection, language prefill, cached decode, and serving reuse.
checkpoint_notes.optimize: Select attention, compile, memory, cache, batching, spatial, or research branches only after their own gates.
checkpoint_notes.publish: Keep Apple MLX, third-party MLX-VLM, paper-only research, and local reproduction states visibly distinct.

optimization_families.title: Evaluation and scheduling
optimization_families.bottleneck: Lazy graph lifetime, synchronization, or misplaced evaluation dominates the measured phase.
optimization_families.proof_gate: Parity passes with identical state and the measured region realizes the intended work exactly once.
optimization_families.rollback: Remove the boundary change when it increases memory, synchronization, or end-to-end latency.

optimization_families.title: Native operators and compilation
optimization_families.bottleneck: A compatible repeated operator or stable graph region dominates runtime.
optimization_families.proof_gate: Eager parity exists and the native or compiled path matches it across representative shapes and state.
optimization_families.rollback: Use the readable eager fallback on semantic mismatch, retracing instability, or no end-to-end benefit.

optimization_families.title: Layout and numerical representation
optimization_families.bottleneck: Dispatch layout, gather structure, dtype, or accumulation behavior limits the target path.
optimization_families.proof_gate: Named axes and numerical roles are explicit and parity plus quality remain within policy.
optimization_families.rollback: Restore the baseline layout or precision when conversion overhead, instability, or quality loss appears.

optimization_families.title: State and memory
optimization_families.bottleneck: Weights or KV state exceed the useful memory budget or create avoidable transfer and cache cost.
optimization_families.proof_gate: State update, position, reset, isolation, and representative memory accounting are validated.
optimization_families.rollback: Return to full state or resident weights when parity, quality, privacy, or end-to-end latency regresses.

optimization_families.title: Compression
optimization_families.bottleneck: Weight, state, or token representation dominates memory or bandwidth after baseline parity.
optimization_families.proof_gate: The compressed artifact passes task-specific quality and memory gates on the representative workload.
optimization_families.rollback: Restore the last parity-passing representation when quality, stability, or total latency fails its gate.

optimization_families.title: Inference algorithms
optimization_families.bottleneck: Autoregressive target-model calls dominate after the underlying decoder is correct.
optimization_families.proof_gate: Acceptance semantics, token distribution or exact mode, state, and task quality match the declared policy.
optimization_families.rollback: Use baseline generation when acceptance overhead, incompatibility, or output-quality drift removes the benefit.

optimization_families.title: Serving and pipeline
optimization_families.bottleneck: Batching, preprocessing, cache reuse, streaming, or multi-request scheduling limits the real workload.
optimization_families.proof_gate: Isolation, cache keys, fairness, task quality, workload mix, and end-to-end behavior are validated.
optimization_families.rollback: Disable reuse or scheduling changes on privacy, correctness, starvation, memory, or tail-latency regression.

optimization_families.title: Custom backend work
optimization_families.bottleneck: A proven hot operation has no adequate native MLX path after safer options are exhausted.
optimization_families.proof_gate: A readable fallback passes parity and the custom path is benchmarked over representative shapes and devices.
optimization_families.rollback: Keep or restore the fallback on semantic mismatch, maintenance risk, rejected platform assumptions, or no measured win.

moe-expert-dispatch-and-quantization: quality against the unquantized expert baseline

glossary.definition: Apple's array framework for machine learning on Apple silicon, with lazy evaluation, composable transforms, and unified-memory operation.
The simplest readable MLX implementation used to prove semantics before compilation or specialized optimization.
Deterministic tensors, state, and outputs captured from the pinned source implementation for comparison.
Evidence that source and MLX behavior agree under a declared exact or numerical comparison policy.
The selected architecture-family and capability path that determines runbooks, risks, and proof gates.
Architecture-specific implementation and validation guidance with supported, experimental, and blocked boundaries.
A named intermediate tensor, state, or output used to localize correctness across the port.
The explicit rename, transpose, reshape, split, merge, dtype, and destination contract for source tensors.
Stored attention keys and values reused across autoregressive decoding steps.
The repository's structured weight-map and conversion contract with explicit tensor transforms and coverage.
A small representative input and expected behavior used as a stable end-to-end validation target.
Structured raw evidence for one bounded benchmark or validation run, including its exact context.
An evidence state that has satisfied the repository's provenance, workload, quality, attestation, and scope gates.
Evidence binding a measurement to its command, dependencies, outputs, and trusted verification boundary.
The controlled hardware, software, model, workload, and capability context used for recommendation eligibility.
The proportion and disposition of source checkpoint keys accounted for by the weight-map contract.
The exact model, revision, target, workload, behavior, and evidence scope a claim is allowed to cover.
